examples: Count 1, 2, 3, 4, 5; the last count names the quantity.
G1-L001.rule: Counting advances by one step in the integer sequence; the next number after n is n + 1.
G1-L001.trace_expectation: A counting answer must cite the counting sequence motif and this lesson.

G1-L002.rule: One-digit addition combines two one-digit quantities by counting forward the second amount.
G1-L002.trace_expectation: An addition answer must cite a one-digit addition motif and any counting dependency.

G1-L003.rule: One-digit subtraction removes the second one-digit quantity from the first and counts what remains.
G1-L003.trace_expectation: A subtraction answer must cite a take-away motif and any counting dependency.

G1-L004.rule: Equality is true when both sides name the same value and false otherwise.
G1-L004.trace_expectation: An equality answer must cite the equality comparison motif and the operation used to evaluate each side.

G1-L005.rule: The larger number comes later in the counting sequence; the smaller number comes earlier.
G1-L005.trace_expectation: A comparison answer must cite the number-order motif and any place-value dependency for two-digit numbers.

examples: Mia has 2 shells and finds 3 more. How many shells now? Answer 5.
Leo has 6 blocks and gives away 2. How many blocks remain? Answer 4.
G1-L006.rule: Simple word problems map 'more' language to addition and 'give away' or 'fly away' language to subtraction.
G1-L006.trace_expectation: A word-problem answer must cite the language-to-operation motif plus the arithmetic motif used.

g1_count_forward_addition.rule: One-digit addition combines two one-digit quantities by counting forward the second amount.
g1_count_forward_addition.trace_expectation: An addition answer must cite a one-digit addition motif and any counting dependency.

g1_counting_sequence.rule: Counting advances by one step in the integer sequence; the next number after n is n + 1.
g1_counting_sequence.trace_expectation: A counting answer must cite the counting sequence motif and this lesson.

g1_number_order_comparison.rule: The larger number comes later in the counting sequence; the smaller number comes earlier.
g1_number_order_comparison.trace_expectation: A comparison answer must cite the number-order motif and any place-value dependency for two-digit numbers.

g1_same_value_equality.rule: Equality is true when both sides name the same value and false otherwise.
g1_same_value_equality.trace_expectation: An equality answer must cite the equality comparison motif and the operation used to evaluate each side.

g1_take_away_subtraction.rule: One-digit subtraction removes the second one-digit quantity from the first and counts what remains.
g1_take_away_subtraction.trace_expectation: A subtraction answer must cite a take-away motif and any counting dependency.

g1_word_problem_operation_mapping.rule: Simple word problems map 'more' language to addition and 'give away' or 'fly away' language to subtraction.
g1_word_problem_operation_mapping.trace_expectation: A word-problem answer must cite the language-to-operation motif plus the arithmetic motif used.
